stories.headline: Dutch police slam pregnant Palestinian woman to ground at migration centre

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: A video of a Dutch policeman throwing a heavily pregnant woman to the ground has caused outrage. The woman says police attacked her at a migration centre where authorities had detained her Palestinian husband.

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: Europe, which fuels global outrage over every unverified claim about women in Iran and calls attackers on police "protesters," just violently slammed a pregnant woman to the ground and dragged her by her hair in the Netherlands — and remains largely silent.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy warns of global missile production crisis as Russia ramps up output

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: There is not enough anti-ballistic missile production in the United States and this could lead to a crisis in different parts of the world. Russia is ramping up its internal production of ballistic missiles.

stories.headline: Senator Rand Paul introduces End Welfare for Non-Citizens Act to prioritize American citizens over illegal immigrants

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: We are nearly $40 trillion in debt. Working families are stretched thin. And Washington is still sending federal benefits to people who entered this country illegally. My End Welfare for Non-Citizens Act supports American citizens instead of rewarding those who came to the U.S. illegally. This is basic fiscal responsibility, and it is long overdue.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: That's a step in the right direction. After we end welfare for non-citizens, let's end it for citizens as well. The general welfare clause of the U.S. Constitution does not include the specific welfare of some at the expense of others.

stories.headline: Guide to changes from Trump's tax and spending cuts package affecting education.
stories.body: President Donald Trump's massive tax and spending cuts package, the "big, beautiful bill" passed by Congress last summer, has brought and will continue to bring changes to paying for education. From expenses for K-12 to higher education loan repayment options and more, many of the shifts take effect soon. Here's a guide to make sure you're ready.

stories.headline: Orange County judge dismisses indictment in dermatologist poisoning case
stories.body:
NEW: Orange County judge has dismissed the indictment against a dermatologist accused of poisoning her husband by putting Drano in his tea

Yue “Emily” Yu was arrested in 2022 after being accused of pouring Drano into her husband’s drinks during their divorce

Her husband, Jack Chen, said he noticed a chemical taste in his lemon tea and set up surveillance cameras, which allegedly captured Yu pouring drain cleaner into a container in the kitchen

FBI testing later found drain cleaner in a drinking glass. Chen claimed he had videos showing three separate poisoning attempts

Yu's attorneys argued the Drano was being used to kill ants and disputed that it caused his medical issues

The case was dismissed after prosecutors denied a jury request to review evidence supporting Yu's defense
